title: Malus × pulcherrima
taxonomy: Malus × domestica, a parent of the Malus × pulcherrima, is a species of apple, but the exact classification of the Malus × pulcherrima is not well-documented in my current knowledge. However, Malus × domestica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a member of the genus Malus, which includes many species of apple. The classification of Malus × pulcherrima specifically falls under the genus Malus and the family Rosaceae. Further research may be needed to determine its exact classification within the genus.
constituents: Malus × pulcherrima contains flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids which are responsible for its bioactive properties. These compounds are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in Malus × pulcherrima are primarily kaempferol and quercetin. These phytochemicals contribute to the plant's potential health benefits and medicinal uses. Research on Malus × pulcherrima continues to explore its active constituents and their effects on human health.
parts: I couldn't find any information about the plant Malus × pulcherrima. However, I know of a plant called Malus domestica, commonly known as the flowering crabapple. Malus × domestica's bark, leaves, and fruits are used in various herbal remedies. The bark is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and fruits are used to make teas and infusions to treat coughs, colds, and skin problems.
